hypothesis: If universal machine learning interatomic potentials are applied to metal–organic frameworks, then they will reproduce DFT-referenced structural optimization results (including lattice volumes), MD stability in NpT simulations (volume changes at 300 K and 1 bar), bulk moduli under ±4% volumetric strain, and heat capacities at 300 K within predefined error margins across a diverse MOF dataset because these MLIPs capture transferable interatomic interactions across MOF chemistries.
